projectScope.activities:
Access

Helicopter from Dawson City or a suitable clearing along the Klondike Highway north of Barlow Lake

Up 20 flights total

“Flying in Caribou Country” and “Flying in Sheep Country” guidelines followed whenever possible

Clearing

Up to 710 m2 on left bank and 400 m2 on right bank

Only 80 m2 of low vegetation removed, of which 72 m2 will be reclaimed

Only tall vegetation removed within 5 m of Little South Klondike River
The Environmental Protection Officer (EPO) will be consulted prior to any clearing > 25 m2

For clearing in areas < 25 m2, trees will be surveyed for wildlife nests and cavities, and any identified features will trigger a stop work policy and notification of the EPO or a qualified professional


Cableway Reconstruction Activities

Replacement of double-plate anchor system on both banks

Excavation: up to 5 x 2.5 x 2.5 m to remove each anchor
Triple-plate anchor system installed on left bank
Double-plate anchor system installed on right bank

Timber infrastructure upgrades on both banks

Excavation: up to 1.5 x 1.5 x 1.5 m to remove existing footings
Reconstruction of timber crib footings for the approximately 6 m high steel A-frame on left bank
Construction of an approximately 3 m high A-frame with timber crib footings on right bank

Reconstruction of cables and utilities

Personnel

Up to six work crew members
No camp proposed

Heavy Equipment Use

All equipment and tools inspected for leaks, kept clean of foreign materials and maintained in good working order

Fuel Use and Handling

Fuel brought to site on an as-needed basis in sealed portable fuel containers

Stored ≥ 30 m from any watercourse
No overnight storage

All refuelling conducted > 30 m from any watercourse
All operating equipment will have secondary containment trays with 110% capacity when stationary
Small equipment operating within 30 m of watercourse will have secondary containment installed with a minimum 125% capacity
Spill kits available with all machinery
Spill Prevention and Emergency Response Plan in place

Waste Management

Garbage and construction waste removed from site daily
All hazardous waste stored in leak-proof containers in an area with secondary containment, protected from the elements and > 30 m from any watercourse
Hazardous and non-hazardous waste is disposed of at an approved facility or recycled
Any human feces will be buried a minimum 20 cm deep and a minimum 70 m from any watercourse

Toilet paper will be removed from site


Remediation

Topsoil and root zone stripped and stored separately for revegetation purposes

Revegetation may also involve planting native clippings and re-seeding with approved seed mixture

Slope stabilized and site restored to natural conditions

Other

No in-stream work proposed

Erosion and sediment control best management practices implemented

To the extent possible, project activities are scheduled for reduced-risk work windows for wildlife
A stop work policy will apply if caribou, moose or bears are encountered
Disturbance to vegetation, especially lichen, shall be minimized.
Personnel will have bear aware training and know how to use bear deterrents
Existing structures will be inspected for nesting birds and bats prior to decommissioning
Should a suspected archeological site or burial ground be discovered, the Proponent will contact the Government of Yukon and the First Nations in the area
projectScope.summary: The Project is the reconstruction of the Little South Klondike River Below Ross Creek (09EA005) hydrometric cableway station to ensure safe and accurate data collection. The ongoing operation of the hydrometric cableway station is indefinite and does not form part of this assessment. Reconstruction activities are expected to take up to 10 days between April and October, within the next five years. The Project is located approximately 90 km east of Dawson City within the Territories of the First Nation of Na-Cho Nyak Dun (FNNND) and Tr’ondëk Hwëch’in (TH). All project activities on the left bank will occur on Settlement Land parcel TH R-2A. Except for access clearing on the left bank, reconstruction activities will occur within the limits of Environment and Climate Change Canada (ECCC) administrative reserve disposition 115P13-012.

Use of mobile crusher, crushing up to 100 tons of material/day, no water use

Minimum 1 000 tons of non-mineralized material will be used to backfill trenches
projectScope.summary: The Project is the amendment to an existing Class 3 Quartz Exploration program (LQ00535) on the Klaza Property on 482 claims. The Project was previously assessed under YESAB Project 2020-0187. The proposed amendment includes the addition of a bulk sample program and associated activities on claims Klaza 1 and Klaza 30, which are captured below. Access will be via Mount Nansen Road and other existing access. The Project is located approximately 51 km west of the Village of Carmacks within the Territory of Little Salmon/Carmacks First Nation. Project activities are proposed from May 5 to November 30 for two years.

projectScope.summary: The Project is a placer mine on 33 claims along McNeill Gulch and Lightning Creek, in the Mayo River Watershed. The Project is located approximately 6 km from Keno City and 50 km from Mayo in the Territory of the First Nation of Na-Cho Nyak Dun. The Project will be accessed via Duncan Creek/Lightning Creek Road. Project activities will occur annually from April 15 to October 28, for 10 years.
